text: Maps have been essential tools for human navigation and understanding the world for thousands of years. They serve as visual representations of geographic areas, allowing people to comprehend spatial relationships, plan routes, and locate places of interest. From ancient hand-drawn charts to modern digital maps powered by GPS and satellite imagery, maps continue to evolve and become more detailed and accessible. Today, maps are embedded in nearly every smartphone and web browser, making it easier than ever for people to explore unfamiliar territories, understand the layout of cities, or plan trips across continents. Whether used for exploration, education, business planning, or simple daily navigation, maps remain one of the most practical and important tools we have for making sense of the physical world around us.
